parts.name: Z-Back Carriage Half A (bearing side)
parts.function: Open carriage half with bearing pockets — receives 4 LM8UU bearings. Z-Back axis.

parts.name: Z-Back Carriage Half B (clamping side)
parts.function: Clamping carriage half — bolts onto half A to capture bearings. Z-Back axis.

parts.name: Z-Front Carriage Half A (bearing side)
parts.function: Open carriage half with bearing pockets — receives 4 LM8UU bearings. Z-Front axis.

parts.name: Z-Front Carriage Half B (clamping side)
parts.function: Clamping carriage half — bolts onto half A to capture bearings. Z-Front axis.

parts.name: 8mm Smooth Test Rod
parts.function: Smooth 8mm steel rod used to test bearing alignment — slide vertically through seated bearings to check for even resistance.

assemblies.name: Batch Carriage Build — All 4 Axes
assemblies.description: Builds all four axis carriages in one production-line session before individual axis bench work begins. Lays out all eight carriage halves at once, then assembles each carriage sequentially: seat bearings, run shake and rod-slide tests, close halves, bolt and tighten. Produces four completed carriages — Y-Left, Y-Right, Z-Back, and Z-Front — ready to be threaded onto guide rods during per-axis bench builds.
assemblies.learningFocus: Production-line carriage assembly: seat four LM8UU bearings per carriage with flanges out, verify fit with shake and rod-slide tests before closing, align belt holes correctly before bolting, and build all four carriages in one session to establish muscle memory before individual axis bench work.

subassemblies.name: All Axis Carriages — Batch Build
subassemblies.description: Batch assembly of all four axis carriages: Y-Left, Y-Right, Z-Back, and Z-Front. All eight carriage halves are laid out together. Each carriage is built sequentially using the same procedure: seat four LM8UU bearings with flanges facing out, shake test, rod-slide test, close halves, insert M6x18 (top) and M6x30 (bottom) bolts, tighten with power drill. Z-Back and Z-Front use a combined-half carriage model.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: All four axis carriages assembled: bearings seated, belt holes aligned, halves bolted. Ready for individual axis bench builds.

guidance.instructionText: Gather and lay out all six carriage pieces on the workbench: Y-Left half A, Y-Left half B, Y-Right half A, Y-Right half B, Z-Back carriage (single piece), Z-Front carriage (single piece). Also set out the hardware for each Y-axis carriage: M6×18 bolts (top holes), M6×30 bolts (bottom holes), and M6 nuts.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Building all four carriages in one session creates muscle memory for the bearing-seating technique before moving to individual axis builds. Laying everything out first lets you spot missing parts before work begins.

steps.name: Clean excess plastic from all carriage holes
steps.instructionText: Use a box cutter or cordless power drill to clear excess plastic from all semi-circle rod holes in each carriage half-piece. Work through all 8 halves before moving on. Holes must be smooth so bearings seat fully and rods insert freely.

steps.name: QC: verify all carriage holes are clean
steps.instructionText: Check every carriage half: each half should have 4 clean semi-circle rod holes, and the M6 bolt holes must be clear of excess plastic. Run a bolt through each hole by hand to confirm clearance before starting the bearing assembly.

steps.name: Carriage 1 (Y-Left): place 4 LM8UU bearings
steps.instructionText: Place four LM8UU linear bearings into one Y-Left carriage half-piece. Flanges must face outward — away from each other — so they sit flush in the lip of each pocket.

steps.name: Carriage 1: close the second carriage half
steps.instructionText: Place the second Y-Left carriage half onto the first, trapping the 4 bearings in their pockets. The small ribbed belt hole should sit directly beside the large smooth belt hole.

guidance.instructionText: Hold both Y-Left carriage halves firmly together and shake the assembly.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Compress the two halves by hand until there is no gap. Shake side-to-side and up-down for 2–3 seconds. No tools needed — this is a hand-compression test only.

validation.successCriteria: No audible rattle or tactile movement from the bearings.
validation.failureCriteria: Bearings click, rattle, or shift inside the halves when shaken.

feedback.successMessage: Bearings are snug. Proceed to the rod slide test.
feedback.failureMessage: Bearing fit is too loose. Swap to a slightly larger LM8UU or try the next batch of bearings. Re-test before closing the carriage.

guidance.instructionText: Insert a smooth guide rod through the Y-Left carriage and hold it so the rod is vertical.
guidance.whyItMattersText: The rod should slide or drop through with slight resistance — if you have to shake the carriage for the rod to move through, that's correct. Motors pull 20 lbs of belt force; the carriage must not slip too freely.

validation.successCriteria: Rod slides or drops through with slight resistance. Shaking the carriage helps it move through.
validation.failureCriteria: Rod slides too quickly with zero resistance (too free), OR rod jams and requires force to push (too tight).

feedback.successMessage: Resistance feels correct. Proceed to close the carriage halves.
feedback.failureMessage: Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest. Too tight: check bearings are fully seated and halves are not warped.

steps.name: Carriage 1: place bolts and thread nuts
steps.instructionText: Insert 2× M6x18 bolts into the two top holes and 2× M6x30 bolts into the two bottom holes. Thread one M6 nut onto each bolt finger-tight.

steps.name: Carriage 1: tighten — 1 of 4 complete
steps.instructionText: Use the power drill on the lowest torque setting to tighten all 4 bolts on the Y-Left carriage. Set it aside — carriage 1 of 4 is complete.

steps.name: Carriage 2 (Y-Right): place 4 LM8UU bearings
steps.instructionText: Pick up the Y-Right carriage halves. Place four LM8UU bearings into half A with flanges facing outward — same technique as carriage 1.

steps.name: Carriage 2: close the second carriage half
steps.instructionText: Place the second Y-Right carriage half onto the first, trapping the 4 bearings. The small ribbed belt hole must sit directly beside the large smooth belt hole.

guidance.instructionText: Hold both Y-Right carriage halves firmly together and shake the assembly.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Compress the two halves by hand until there is no gap. Shake side-to-side and up-down for 2–3 seconds.

validation.successCriteria: No audible rattle or tactile movement from the bearings.
validation.failureCriteria: Bearings click, rattle, or shift inside the halves when shaken.

feedback.successMessage: Bearings are snug. Proceed to the rod slide test.
feedback.failureMessage: Bearing fit is too loose. Swap to a slightly larger LM8UU or try the next batch of bearings.

guidance.instructionText: Insert a smooth guide rod through the Y-Right carriage and hold it vertical.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Slight resistance is correct — the rod should slide or drop through with a little drag. Too free means the carriage will slip under belt tension.

validation.successCriteria: Rod slides or drops through with slight resistance.
validation.failureCriteria: Rod slides with zero resistance (too free), OR jams and requires force (too tight).

feedback.successMessage: Resistance feels correct. Proceed to close the carriage halves.
feedback.failureMessage: Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest. Too tight: check bearings are fully seated.

steps.name: Carriage 2: place bolts and thread nuts
steps.instructionText: Insert 2× M6x18 bolts into the two top holes and 2× M6x30 bolts into the two bottom holes. Thread one M6 nut onto each bolt finger-tight.

steps.name: Carriage 2: tighten — 2 of 4 complete
steps.instructionText: Tighten all 4 bolts on the Y-Right carriage with the power drill on lowest torque. Set it beside carriage 1 — carriage 2 of 4 is complete.

steps.name: Carriage 3 (Z-Back): place 4 LM8UU bearings in half A
steps.instructionText: Pick up the Z-Back carriage half A. Place four LM8UU bearings into the bearing pockets with flanges facing outward. Same technique as the Y-axis carriages.

guidance.instructionText: Hold both Z-Back carriage halves firmly together and shake the assembly.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Compress the halves by hand. Shake side-to-side and up-down for 2–3 seconds. Tip: use one of the completed Y-axis carriages as a tactile reference for correct feel.

validation.successCriteria: No audible rattle or tactile movement from the bearings.
validation.failureCriteria: Bearings click, rattle, or shift inside the halves when shaken.

feedback.successMessage: Bearings are snug. Proceed to the rod slide test.
feedback.failureMessage: Bearing fit is too loose. Swap to a slightly larger LM8UU and re-test before closing.

guidance.instructionText: Insert a smooth guide rod through the Z-Back carriage and hold it vertical.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Slight resistance is correct. The rod should slide or drop through with a little drag, not freely.

validation.successCriteria: Rod slides or drops through with slight resistance.
validation.failureCriteria: Rod slides with zero resistance (too free), OR jams and requires force (too tight).

feedback.successMessage: Resistance feels correct. Proceed to bolt the carriage.
feedback.failureMessage: Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest. Too tight: check bearings are fully seated.

steps.name: Carriage 3: close halves, bolt and tighten — 3 of 4 complete
steps.instructionText: Press Z-Back carriage half B onto half A to trap the bearings. Align so the small ribbed belt hole sits beside the large smooth belt hole. Secure with M6x18 bolts on top and M6x30 bolts on bottom. Tighten with the power drill on lowest torque. Set it aside — carriage 3 of 4 is complete.

steps.name: Carriage 4 (Z-Front): place 4 LM8UU bearings in half A
steps.instructionText: Pick up the Z-Front carriage half A. Place four LM8UU bearings into the bearing pockets with flanges facing outward.

guidance.instructionText: Hold both Z-Front carriage halves firmly together and shake the assembly.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Compress the halves by hand. Shake side-to-side and up-down for 2–3 seconds.

validation.successCriteria: No audible rattle or tactile movement from the bearings.
validation.failureCriteria: Bearings click, rattle, or shift inside the halves when shaken.

feedback.successMessage: Bearings are snug. Proceed to the rod slide test.
feedback.failureMessage: Bearing fit is too loose. Swap to a slightly larger LM8UU and re-test.

guidance.instructionText: Insert a smooth guide rod through the Z-Front carriage and hold it vertical.
guidance.whyItMattersText: Slight resistance is correct. The rod should slide or drop through with a little drag.

validation.successCriteria: Rod slides or drops through with slight resistance.
validation.failureCriteria: Rod slides with zero resistance (too free), OR jams and requires force (too tight).

feedback.successMessage: Resistance feels correct. Proceed to bolt the carriage.
feedback.failureMessage: Too free: wrap each bearing in one layer of electrical tape and retest. Too tight: check bearings are fully seated.

steps.name: Carriage 4: close halves, bolt and tighten — 4 of 4 complete
steps.instructionText: Press Z-Front carriage half B onto half A to trap the bearings. Align the small ribbed belt hole directly beside the large smooth belt hole. Secure with M6x18 bolts on top and M6x30 bolts on bottom. Tighten with the power drill on lowest torque. All four axis carriages are now complete — set the Z-Front carriage with the others.
